title: Experience Pool
body: Your Experience Pool is your library of jobs and the tasks (bullet points) you did in each one. Build it up on the Experience Pool page — add jobs, then add tasks under each job.

title: Task versions
body: Each task can have multiple versions — different ways of phrasing the same bullet point, tailored to different kinds of roles. One version is marked as the default, which is used when you add the task to a CV. You can switch between versions for a task in the Pool Drawer while editing a CV.

title: Profiles
body: A profile is a named variant of your CV — for example 'Tech Lead' or 'Data Science' — used to group together CVs aimed at a particular kind of role. Profiles are managed in the CV Library.

title: Cover letters
body: Cover letters aren't stored separately — each job application in the Application Tracker has its own cover letter, which you can write, generate with AI, or copy from a previous application.

title: The Pool Drawer
body: The Pool Drawer is the panel that slides out in Assembly (the CV editor) showing your whole Experience Pool. Use it to toggle tasks in or out of the CV you're building, and to switch which version of a task is used.

title: Finding your way around
body: The sidebar has these sections: Home (your dashboard), Experience Pool, Education & Skills, Personal Details, CV Library, Assembly (the CV editor), Reference Letters, Application Tracker, Import, Help, and Settings.

title: Importing your data
body: The Import page is the fastest way to get your data into CV Ferret if you already have a CV. The AI path accepts a DOCX, PDF, Markdown, or plain text file — or you can paste your CV text directly — and Claude extracts your jobs, tasks, education, skills, languages, and personal details automatically. The manual path lets you download a JSON template, fill it in with your own data using any text editor, and upload it; no AI required. Both paths lead to the same review screen where you can edit or remove anything before it's saved.

title: Getting started
body: If you already have a CV, the quickest start is the Import page — the AI path can extract everything from a DOCX or PDF in one go, or use the manual JSON path if you prefer not to use AI. If you're starting from scratch or prefer to enter data directly, the suggested order is: Personal Details, then Experience Pool (jobs and tasks), then Education & Skills. Once your pool is built, create a CV in CV Library and Assembly, and track your job applications in the Application Tracker.
